- Gujarat Rain Alert: 11 Districts Forecasted, Rajkot Temp Drops | Storm Warning
Pre-monsoon showers bring relief from intense heat:Rain forecast issued for 11 districts; Rajkot temperature drops 3.7°C as storm alert remains in place
- Copy link
After days of intense heat across Gujarat, the weather has taken a dramatic turn, with heavy rainfall lashing parts of Aravalli and Mahisagar districts since early Friday (May 29) morning following forecasts issued by the Meteorological Department.
Dark rain-bearing clouds and widespread showers brought much-needed relief from the scorching conditions, leading to a noticeable drop in temperatures and a sudden spell of cooler weather across several regions of the state.
Rajkot records a sharp fall of 3.7°C in its maximum temperature
The impact of the changing weather was reflected in temperature readings, with Rajkot recording a sharp fall of 3.7°C in its maximum temperature. Despite the drop, Ahmedabad remained the hottest city in Gujarat, registering a maximum temperature of 43.1°C.
The Meteorological Department has now warned of unstable weather conditions across the state over the next six days, forecasting thunderstorms, strong winds and intermittent rainfall in multiple districts.
Authorities have issued a special advisory to farmers
In view of the forecast, authorities have issued a special advisory to farmers, urging them to move standing crops, harvested produce and fodder to safer locations to minimise potential losses from rain and gusty winds.
Weather officials have indicated that pre-monsoon activity is likely to remain active over the coming days, bringing further relief from the severe heat that has gripped Gujarat in recent weeks.
Temperatures fall by up to 4°C across Gujarat in 24 hours
A marked decline in daytime temperatures has been recorded across several parts of the state over the past 24 hours, providing relief from the intense heatwave conditions.
Rajkot witnessed one of the sharpest drops, with the maximum temperature falling by 3.7°C from 43.6°C to 39.9°C.
Surendranagar also recorded a significant decline, with temperatures dropping by 3.2°C from 44.7°C to 41.5°C. The biggest fall was reported in Deesa, where the maximum temperature plunged by 4.1°C, from 42.2°C to 38.1°C.
Elsewhere, Amreli recorded a drop of 2.4°C, with temperatures falling from 41.9°C to 39.5°C. At Kandla Airport, the mercury declined by 2.4°C from 40°C to 37.6°C, while Kandla town recorded a decrease of 1.3°C, from 37.8°C to 36.5°C.
The drop in temperatures is being attributed to increased cloud cover, rainfall activity and changing weather conditions associated with the ongoing pre-monsoon system affecting the state.
In the state capital Gandhinagar, the maximum temperature fell by 0.8°C, dropping from 43.5°C to 42.7°C, while Ahmedabad recorded a decline of 1.3°C, with the mercury easing from 44.4°C to 43.1°C, offering residents some relief from the intense heat.
Most other districts also recorded a moderate fall in temperatures. However, Daman and Porbandar were the only two locations in the state where temperatures registered a slight increase instead of a decline.
Overall, a noticeable drop in temperatures has been recorded across almost all inland regions, particularly in Saurashtra and North Gujarat, while coastal areas experienced relatively smaller changes.
Weather officials have indicated that the cooling trend is likely to continue. Over the next seven days, maximum temperatures across Gujarat are expected to decline gradually by a further 3°C to 5°C as pre-monsoon weather activity intensifies across the state.
Heavy rain lashes Aravalli after prolonged heatwave
Aravalli district witnessed a dramatic change in weather conditions on Friday (May 29) morning as heavy rainfall swept across several parts of the region, bringing much-needed relief after days of intense heat and humidity.
In line with forecasts issued by the Meteorological Department, unseasonal rain is being reported in multiple areas of the district for today (May 30). Heavy showers were recorded in district headquarters Modasa as well as in Malpur and Bhiloda talukas.
Rainfall was also reported from the popular pilgrimage town of Shamlaji, where cloudy skies and showers led to a noticeable drop in temperatures.
The sudden spell of rain has brought cooler conditions across the district, ending a prolonged period of scorching weather and providing significant relief to residents.
The impact of the rainfall was also felt across several rural parts of Aravalli district. Apart from Malpur town, substantial rainfall was reported from Vasherakampa and Sunokh villages in Bhiloda taluka, as well as Isarol (Isari), Jitpur and Jivanpur in the Modasa region.
These parts of North Gujarat had been experiencing persistently high temperatures and severe heat conditions for several days. However, the sudden spell of unseasonal rain has led to a noticeable fall in temperatures across the region.
The heavy showers have brought a welcome change in weather conditions, with cooler winds and overcast skies replacing the intense heat. Residents said the rainfall has provided significant relief from the prolonged spell of scorching temperatures and humidity.
Rain brings relief to Mahisagar after days of intense heat
Mahisagar district also witnessed a significant shift in weather conditions on Friday morning, in line with forecasts issued by the Meteorological Department.
Dark clouds covered the skies across the district from the early hours of the day, including district headquarters Lunawada, before light pre-monsoon showers began in several areas.
Intermittent rainfall was reported from Lunawada city and surrounding rural regions, while cloudy conditions accompanied rain showers in other parts of the district, including Khanpur, Santrampur and Kadana talukas.
The change in weather has brought considerable relief to residents who had been struggling with rising temperatures and oppressive heat over the past several days. The rainfall and cooler conditions have led to a noticeable improvement in the overall atmosphere across the district.
Forecast of heavy rain and storms for the next 6 days
Ahmedabad likely to remain hot despite weather changes elsewhere
While several parts of Gujarat are witnessing rain and a fall in temperatures, Ahmedabad is expected to continue experiencing predominantly clear skies over the next few days, according to the local weather forecast.
The city's maximum temperature is likely to remain around 43°C, indicating that heatwave-like conditions may persist despite increased pre-monsoon activity in other regions of the state.
On Friday (May 29), Ahmedabad recorded a maximum temperature of 43.1°C, making it one of the hottest locations in Gujarat. Weather officials, however, expect temperatures to gradually ease in the coming days as broader atmospheric conditions across the state continue to change.
